title: When Insomnia Cost Me Everything

story.opening: <break time="500ms"/>I lost my job because I couldn't sleep. <break time="400ms"/>The studio I worked at for eight years.
story.body: <break time="450ms"/>At first I hid it. <break time="400ms"/>Coffee. <break time="350ms"/>Energy drinks. <break time="300ms"/>Pushing through. <break time="500ms"/>But you can't hide chronic sleep deprivation forever. <break time="400ms"/>I made mistakes. <break time="350ms"/>Missed deadlines. <break time="300ms"/>My work quality dropped. <break time="500ms"/>They were understanding at first. <break time="400ms"/>Then concerned. <break time="350ms"/>Then frustrated. <break time="600ms"/>By the end, I was barely functional. <break time="500ms"/>The conversation was gentle, but the message was clear: <break time="400ms"/>I couldn't do the job anymore.
story.reflection: <break time="600ms"/>Losing that job felt like losing myself. <break time="500ms"/>But it also forced me to stop pretending. <break time="400ms"/>To get serious about treatment. <break time="350ms"/>To rebuild my life around sleep, not against it. <break time="600ms"/>If you're scared of losing things - <break time="500ms"/>I understand. <break time="400ms"/>That fear is real. <break time="350ms"/>But treating the problem matters more than hiding it.

when_to_share: When someone fears insomnia is destroying their life or work
